problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing
hypothesis: The key to Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? is Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing — specifically Fear memory reconsolidation — which would help 12,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Longitudinal patient cohort with 9 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The art of science is asking the right question, not answering. — Max Planck paraphrase
